chapter_title.en: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable

parts.text: وَقَالَ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كُلُّ طَرِيقٍ يُوطَأُ

text: هِ جَادَّةٌ أَوْ لَمْ تَكُنْ لاَ يَنْبَغِي اَلصَّلاَةُ فِيهِ» قِيلَ فَأَيْنَ يُصَلَّى قَالَ «يَمْنَةً وَ يَسْرَةً.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.728 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
"Any path that is trodden and commonly used, whether it has a main road or not, should not be used for prayer."
He was asked: "Then where should one pray?"
Imam (as) said: "To the right or to the left (of the path)."
